skill.name: clinvar-database
skill.description: Query NCBI ClinVar for human genetic variant classifications, review status, data formats, API access, FTP downloads, and VCF annotation workflows.
skill.summary: Guide ClinVar searches, bulk data access, and evidence-aware variant interpretation.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command execution and network patterns, but review shows they are Markdown examples for public NCBI ClinVar access rather than executable skill code. The main confirmed concern is that the documentation includes networked shell commands, including a remote installer pattern, so publication is acceptable with a warning about user approval before running commands.

medium_findings.title: Networked Shell Commands in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill includes Markdown examples for curl, wget, bcftools, Entrez Direct, and FTP downloads. These are legitimate ClinVar workflows, but an assistant should not execute them without user approval because they contact external NCBI services and may download large files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in Markdown code blocks and perform public ClinVar data access. Context supports legitimate use, but external command execution and network downloads remain real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Remote Installer Command Example
medium_findings.description: The API reference shows a shell command that downloads an Entrez Direct installer and executes it with sh. This is a common installation pattern, but it is riskier than ordinary query examples because it executes remote script content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution and shell execution pattern is explicit. The endpoint is NCBI and the use case is legitimate, so this is a cautionary finding rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Public NCBI URLs Are Expected
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to NCBI, ClinVar, ClinGen, Samtools, and related public documentation or data resources. No evidence found of credential exfiltration, tracking endpoints, or unrelated third-party callbacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs match the stated ClinVar data-access purpose and are visible documentation links. I did not find evidence of hidden or suspicious network destinations.

low_findings.title: API Key Placeholder Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The API key reference uses a placeholder for NCBI rate limits and does not read environment variables or include a real secret. This dismisses the env_access static finding as a false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text instructs users to add a placeholder API key parameter. There is no code path that accesses environment variables or transmits stored credentials.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts appear on prose, headings, or biomedical terminology rather than cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak algorithms being used for security decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation text at these locations, not cryptographic operations. The pattern match is likely caused by domain terms or Markdown content.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Data Processing Examples
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance labels map to examples such as parsing JSON, filtering files, and listing public FTP content. No evidence found of host enumeration, port scanning, or local system profiling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is ClinVar query and file-processing documentation. The flagged lines do not show reconnaissance intent or collection of local system details.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Script Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: A documented installer command pipes downloaded content into a shell. Users should verify the source and approve execution before running this pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line contains an explicit sh command with command substitution around a network download. The risk is confirmed, although the documented source is a legitimate NCBI resource.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bulk Network Download Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The data format reference includes shell scripts that download ClinVar XML, VCF, and tab-delimited files from FTP. These can consume storage and bandwidth and should run only after user confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts are visible examples for public data retrieval. They are not hidden or malicious, but they do perform external downloads.

content.value_statement: Variant interpretation requires current ClinVar context and careful review status checks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through ClinVar queries, bulk downloads, and evidence-aware summaries.
actual_capabilities: Build ClinVar searches by gene, condition, variant name, chromosome, and clinical significance.
Explain ClinVar clinical significance terms, review status ratings, and conflict handling.
Guide E-utilities, Entrez Direct, FTP, XML, VCF, and tab-delimited data access.
Select appropriate ClinVar file formats for research, pipelines, or database loading.
Outline VCF annotation and filtering workflows with ClinVar clinical significance fields.
Emphasize version tracking, access dates, and professional review for clinical contexts.
limitations: It does not provide medical diagnosis or replace a genetics professional.
It depends on external NCBI services and ClinVar update schedules.
ClinVar VCF files exclude large and complex variants over 10 kb.
Bulk XML releases can be large and may require local bioinformatics tooling.

use_cases.title: Review a Gene Variant List
use_cases.description: Find ClinVar classifications, review status, and conflict signals for variants in a target gene.

use_cases.title: Annotate Sequencing Results
use_cases.description: Choose the correct ClinVar VCF build and plan filtering for pathogenic or uncertain variants.

use_cases.title: Build a Reproducible Dataset
use_cases.description: Select monthly ClinVar releases, document access dates, and compare XML, VCF, and tabular formats.

prompt_templates.title: Search One Gene
prompt_templates.prompt: Find ClinVar search strategies for pathogenic variants in BRCA1. Explain which review status values I should prioritize.

prompt_templates.title: Interpret a Variant Result
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the ClinVar meaning of a likely pathogenic variant with one-star review status and conflicting submissions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan VCF Annotation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have GRCh38 VCF data. Show how to choose ClinVar files and which INFO fields to retain for clinical significance review.

prompt_templates.title: Design Bulk Data Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reproducible ClinVar bulk download and parsing workflow that tracks release version, genome build, review status, and conflicts.

output_examples.output: A concise search plan with ClinVar query terms, review status filters, recommended record fields, and warnings about conflicting interpretations.

output_examples.input: Explain ClinVar VCF limitations for a pipeline.
output_examples.output: A plain-language summary covering genome build matching, missing large variants, key INFO fields, and version documentation.

output_examples.input: Compare XML, VCF, and tab-delimited ClinVar files.
output_examples.output: A format selection guide that maps each file type to evidence review, pipeline annotation, or quick statistical analysis.

best_practices: Record ClinVar release version, genome build, and access date for every analysis.
Prioritize expert panel or practice guideline review status when summarizing evidence.
Ask for confirmation before running network downloads or shell commands from examples.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a VUS classification as a clinical diagnosis.
Do not mix GRCh37 and GRCh38 coordinates without explicit conversion checks.
Do not ignore conflicting interpretations or low review status in clinical summaries.

faq.question: Can this skill diagnose a patient?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes ClinVar information and workflow guidance, but clinical decisions require qualified genetics review.

faq.question: Does it access live ClinVar data automatically?
faq.answer: It provides query and download guidance. Live access depends on the user approving network requests or using external tools.

faq.question: Which ClinVar format should I use for VCF annotation?
faq.answer: Use the ClinVar VCF that matches your genome build, such as GRCh37 or GRCh38.

faq.question: When should I use XML instead of VCF?
faq.answer: Use XML when you need complete submission details, evidence, conditions, and record history.

faq.question: Why does review status matter?
faq.answer: Review status indicates evidence strength and submitter consensus. Higher ratings usually support greater confidence.

faq.question: Can it handle conflicting interpretations?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to identify conflicts and recommends manual evidence review before drawing conclusions.
